symptoms.character: feels full after two bites; cannot finish normal meals

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Early satiety and postprandial fullness are the cardinal features of the postprandial-distress form of functional dyspepsia.
Persistent early satiety can rarely signal obstruction or malignancy — one reason endoscopy is needed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe constant epigastric burning pain, not meal-predictable
symptoms.character: constant burning, poorly relieved by antacids

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Epigastric pain syndrome is a functional-dyspepsia subtype; the non-meal-locked pattern fits.
Severe epigastric burning unresponsive to antacids in an NSAID user is equally consistent with an ulcer — indistinguishable without endoscopy.
NSAIDs impair mucosal prostaglandin defence and are a leading cause of peptic ulceration and upper GI bleeding.
An identifiable NSAID cause of mucosal injury argues against a purely functional label until endoscopy excludes an ulcer.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild nausea, frequent belching and upper-abdominal bloating

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nausea, belching and bloating commonly accompany functional dyspepsia and impaired gastric accommodation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No relief of symptoms with defecation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Lack of relief with defecation argues against IBS and keeps the focus on the upper GI tract.
Absence of overt bleeding lowers the probability of a complicated ulcer but does not exclude an uncomplicated one.

symptoms.symptom_name: No dysphagia and no unintentional weight loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of these alarm features lowers malignancy concern but does not remove the indication for endoscopy in an NSAID user.

symptoms.symptom_name: Epigastric pain non-radiating; no diaphoresis or breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-radiating pain without autonomic features lowers ACS probability but cannot exclude it without an ECG.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake and weakness for 48 hours

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sustained poor intake with weakness raises dehydration risk and drives same-day rather than routine assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised epigastric tenderness with no guarding, rigidity, rebound; negative Murphy's; normal bowel sounds

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Localised epigastric tenderness without peritonism fits an uncomplicated upper GI process — a clinician exam finding the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.
Absence of peritonism argues against perforation in person — but this is exactly the assessment telehealth cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (afebrile, not tachycardic, normotensive)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured normal observations lower the immediate-emergency probability, but were taken in person and are not available to the AI Doctor over chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Upper GI endoscopy (presumed normal — the confirmatory test)

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal mucosa (no ulcer, no malignancy) — the defining feature confirming functional dyspepsia
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without endoscopy an NSAID ulcer or malignancy cannot be excluded and functional dyspepsia cannot be confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal endoscopy is the criterion that distinguishes functional dyspepsia from organic upper GI disease.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these define the functional-dyspepsia symptom complex — but only once organic disease is excluded by endoscopy.
Severe antacid-resistant epigastric burning in a daily NSAID user is the key organic mimic that forbids functional closure and mandates NSAID cessation plus endoscopy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms functional dyspepsia and excludes NSAID ulcer/malignancy in a daily NSAID user with severe dyspepsia.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Same-day in-person clinical assessment with vital signs +/- bloods (FBC, U&E, lipase, LFTs)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Compensates for the abdominal exam, hydration assessment and bloods the AI Doctor cannot perform; screens for anaemia, dehydration and biliary/pancreatic causes.
Standard in the work-up of dyspepsia; test-and-treat where positive.
Cheap exclusion of atypical cardiac ischaemia presenting as epigastric pain — obtained at the in-person assessment, not remotely.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Abdominal imaging for routine functional dyspepsia without red flags
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Cross-sectional imaging is not first-line for uncomplicated dyspepsia; endoscopy is the appropriate test. Avoid over-investigation.
